UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS THAI CHIU AND KAMAN LIU'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' JOINT MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS NOT BASED ON NEW/DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW. ALL THE AUTHORITIES CITED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION WERE AVAILABLE TO THE DEFENDANT AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN ISSUED AND BEFORE THE ORAL ARGUMENT. ADDITIONALLY, AS IN THE ORIGINAL MOTION DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE COMPLAINT IS PRESENTED FOR IMPROPER PURPOSE, THAT THE CLAIMS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE NOT WARRANTED BY EXISTING LAW, THAT FACTUAL CONTENTIONS ARE NOT LIKELY TO HAVE EVIDENTIARY SUPPORT AFTER A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY FOR DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 (B). JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI OBJECTION TO DEFENDANTS UNDERTAKING IS SUSTAINED. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS INSUFFICIENT POSTING OF SECURITY TO PERFECT AN APPEAL. IF NO NEW SECURITY IS POSTED, OR IF OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED, THE COURT WILL GRANT THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND ISSUE AN ORDER STATING NO FURTHER EFFORTS TO POST SECURITY ALLOWED. THE STAY IS LIFTED AS OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. COUNSEL AGREES TO THE FOLLOWING BRIEFING SCHEDULE FOR THE MOTION TO COMPEL POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY ANSWERS, AND FOR SANCTIONS: DEFENDANTS MUST CORRECT THE POSTING OF SECURITY BY FEBRUARY 14, 2017; PLAINTIFFS OBJECTION MUST BE FILED BY FEBRUARY 22, 2017; AND THE HEARING OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL REMAINS SCHEDULED FOR MARCH 2, 2017. DEFENDANT THAI MING CHUI'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT BEARS THE BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT THE FUNDS ARE EXEMPT. (SEE CCP SEC. 703.580(B); O'BRIEN V. THE SHERIFF IS ORDERED TO REMIT $9,004.89 TO DEFENDANT CHUI. THE REMAINING FUNDS IN THE ACCOUNT SHALL BE DISTRIBUTED TO THE CREDITOR PLAINTIFF. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, INTERVENOR BRYANT FU, PAUL LIANG JOINT MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE PURPORTED PURPOSE FOR THE INTERVENTION (SEEKING APPROVAL OF THE SHERIFF'S SALE) IS NOT SUPPORTED BY AUTHORITY. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE COMPANION MOTION TO ACCEPT AUCTION BID FOR JUDGMENT DEBTOR TINA YAN'S DWELLING CONTAINED NO EVIDENCE THAT WOULD ALLOW THE ACCEPTANCE OF THE LOWER BID, I.E. EVIDENCE REGARDING THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE BID TO COVER LI'S JUDGMENT, OUTSTANDING SENIOR DEBTS AND THE HOMESTEAD, LET ALONE ANY ADDITIONAL FUNDS TO BE DISBURSED TO FU. FURTHERMORE, THERE IS NO AUTHORITY WHATSOEVER CITED FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE BIDDER HAS STANDING TO SEEK THE RELIEF OF BID ACCEPTANCE. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, SHUZHENTU'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MOVING PARTY MOVES TO EXPUNGE THE LIS PENDENCY ON TWO DISTINCT GROUNDS ACCORDING TO THE NOTICE OF MOTION. AN ADDITIONAL BASIS FOR EXPUNGEMENT IN LISTED IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, BUT NOT IN THE NOTICE. THE LEGAL ARGUMENT IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES OPENS UP WITH REFERENCE TO CCP 405.30, HOWEVER MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH THE ENTIRETY OF THIS CODE SECTION OR CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS EXCUSED FROM COMPLIANCE. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT, BASED ON THE ARGUMENTS ADVANCED BY THE MOVING PARTY, THEIR INTEREST IN THE REAL PROPERTY IS NOT AFFECTED BY THE LIS PENDENS, AS THEIR INTEREST PRE-DATES THE LIS PENDENS AND BECAUSE THE PURPOSE OF THE LIS PENDENS HAS NOW BEEN SATISFIED, PLAINTIFF HAVING OBTAINED A JUDGEMENT. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY CASES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT FAILURE TO FILE A LIS PENDENS WITH THE COURT RESULTS IN A VOID LIS PENDENS. THE SECTION OF THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES REGARDING COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 405.22 IS DEVOID OF ANY CASE CITATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion To Compel Appearance And Testimony Of Vi Dan Tran A.K.A Harry Nguyen At A Deposition, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel Vi Dan Tran, a.k.a. Harry Nguyen ("Tran") to appear for deposition is denied without prejudice. Any attempt to examine Tran should be directed to Department 505.

"All applications for orders for the appearance and examination of judgment debtors or other persons must be in writing and presented to the subsequent filing window in Room 103 pursuant to CCP 708.110 et seq. All such orders must be made returnable to Department 505 on any Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday at 2:00 p.m." (SF Local Rule 8.9A.)

"The Civil Discovery Act (CCP 2016.010 et seq.) applies to discovery in aid of enforcement of a money judgment only to the extent provided by CCP 708.010-708.030." (Cal. Judges Benchbook Civ. Proc. After Trial, sec. 7.2 (2025), emphasis added; see also Cal Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments and Debts, 6:1282 (2025) [third party "can be subpoenaed to testify before the court or a referee in an examination proceeding in the same manner as a trial witness."].)

In Shrewsbury Management, Inc. v. Superior Court (2019) 32 Cal.App.5th 1213, the court held that a party could issue a subpoena duces tecum on a third party to collect a judgment. "We conclude that when a subpoena duces tecum is tethered to an examination under section 708.110, the scope of discoverable documents must be broadly construed to include matters relating to the property and business affairs of the judgment debtor, and is not limited by section 708.120." (Shrewsbury, 32 Cal.App.5th at 1228.) Therefore, even under Shrewsbury, the discovery request needs to be tethered to an examination proceeding and Department 505 conducts those proceedings.

Plaintiff does not present any persuasive authority holding that he can ignore all of the intricacies of CCP 708.010 et seq. by serving a garden variety deposition subpoena on a third-party witness. Any attempt to examine Tran must be made under CCP 708.110 et seq and directed to Department 505.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: SHUZHENTU'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party moves to expunge the lis pendency on two distinct grounds according to the Notice of Motion. An additional basis for expungement in listed in the Points and Authorities, but not in the notice. The legal argument in the Points and Authorities opens up with reference to CCP 405.30, however moving party failed to show compliance with the entirety of this code section or cite any authority for the proposition that the moving party is excused from compliance. The Court also notes that, based on the arguments advanced by the moving party, their interest in the real property is not affected by the lis pendens, as their interest pre-dates the lis pendens and because the purpose of the lis pendens has now been satisfied, Plaintiff having obtained a judgement. Moving party fails to cite any cases for the proposition that failure to file a lis pendens with the Court results in a void lis pendens. The section of the Points and Authorities regarding compliance with CCP 405.22 is devoid of any case citations. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Joint Motion For Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Mar-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

INTERVENOR BRYANT FU, PAUL LIANG JOINT MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE is DENIED without prejudice. The purported purpose for the intervention (seeking approval of the Sheriff's Sale) is not supported by authority. The Court notes that the companion motion to accept auction bid for judgment debtor Tina Yan's dwelling contained no evidence that would allow the acceptance of the lower bid, i.e. evidence regarding the sufficiency of the bid to cover Li's judgment, outstanding senior debts and the homestead, let alone any additional funds to be disbursed to Fu. Furthermore, there is no authority whatsoever cited for the proposition that the bidder has standing to seek the relief of bid acceptance.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

ARGUED. The Court addressed the issue of the unfiled substitution of attorney form. Mr. Disston requested additional time to complete the filing. After a few minutes Mr. Disston reported that he was unable to get his form filed but he will continue to work on it. The Court asked Mr. Disston if he was able to see the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Disston responded no and stated that he saw an order that parties are to appear this morning. Mr. Thai stated that he reviewed the tentative ruling. The Court read aloud the tentative ruling for the benefit of Mr. Disston.
Mr. Thai reported that he was able to read the supplemental declarations filed this morning, that the comparison of the home in the neighborhood and the home of Ms. Yan is not appropriate, that Ms. Yan's property is a two unit apartment building with defects and that opinion is not reasonable. Mr. Disston had no further argument to add and stated that the point brought up by Mr. Thai go to the weight as opinion as to value. The Court offered a suggestion on how to keep this case moving forward. Mr. Thai stated that he will contact the previous appraiser, requested the defendant pre-pay the appraiser and requested this matter be continued for four weeks. The Court stated that the parties may use someone who has already appraised the property.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause (Judgment Debtor Thai Ming Chiu) Why An Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Issue is continued to November 02, 2021 at 9:30am in Dept. 501.. The plaintiff will obtain two appraisals, one for each property, the defendants are ordered to pay for the appraisals and are order to fully cooperate with the appraiser provided that they receive 24 hour notice. HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Mar-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Thai Ming Chui's claim of exemption is granted in part and denied in part.

Defendant bears the burden of demonstrating that the funds are exempt. (See CCP sec. 703.580(b); O'Brien v. AMBS Diagnostics, LLC (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 942, 948.) Defendant demonstrates a total exemption of $9,004.89 ($3,500 under CCP sec. 704.080(b)(2) [social security] and $5,504.89 under CCP sec. 704.110 [public retirement benefits].)

The Sheriff is ordered to remit $9,004.89 to defendant Chui. The remaining funds in the account shall be distributed to the creditor plaintiff.

The Court will prepare a judicial council order reflecting the above.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Duy Thai, Esq. of Intellectual Property Litigation Business Law, 415.296.9927, appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN'S NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER (PURSUANT TO CCP 473 (d)).

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted: Defendant Demas Yan's motion to set aside order appointing receiver is denied. There are three separate and independent reasons requiring denial of this motion. First, Mr. Yan is in default status now and was at the time the motion for appointment of receiver was filed, so he had no right to contest the appointment of a receiver. Second, Mr. Yan's counsel of record, Mr. Boasberg, was served with all relevant papers on a timely basis. Third, Mr. Yan has failed to provide any grounds for opposing the appointment of a receiver. The order is signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Postjudgment Discovery Answers, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Duy Thai, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.296.9927, for plaintiffs Charles Li;
Demas Yan, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.867.5797, for defendants Thai Ming Chiu.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF and judgment creditor CHARLES LI's Amended Motion To Compel Post-judgment Discovery Answers from defendants Thai Ming Chiu, Kaman Liu, Cheuk Tin Yan and Tina Yan and request for monetary sanctions is granted as to Thai Ming Chiu, Kaman Liu and Tina Yan. Defendants Thai Ming Chiu, Kaman Liu and Tina Yan must respond to all discovery requests made of them fully and without objections and Mr. Li is awarded monetary sanctions of $3500 against all three of them and their counsel Demas Yam. The motion is off calendar as to defendant Cheuk Tin Yan since he is now deceased. The sole basis for failing to respond to the discovery is the defendants' contention that there is a stay pending appeal as a result of purported surety filings made by defendants to bond the judgment. This court has repeatedly and conclusively determined that the purported surety filings are deficient. Defendants' position that the purported personal surety filings effected a stay of postjudgment collection activity lacks substantial justification. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
